name: Community Service Network, Inc.
description_short: Non-profit organization offering free and low-cost homebuyer education, property tax assistance, and financial counseling for low-to-moderate income Massachusetts residents.
description_long:
Community Service Network, Inc. (CSN) is a Massachusetts-based non-profit organization dedicated to serving low-to-moderate income households with housing and financial assistance programs. The organization operates primarily in the Stoneham area but extends services across Massachusetts for certain programs. CSN functions as an educational and advocacy organization rather than a direct lending institution, focusing on helping clients build knowledge and access resources to improve their financial stability.

CSN offers three primary service tracks: the Stoneham Tax Assistance Fund (STAF) program, which provides monetary assistance to cost-burdened homeowners for property taxes; pre-purchase first-time homebuyer education courses available in multiple formats (in-person, webinar, and online hybrid); and advocacy and referral services connecting clients to grants and resources. The homebuyer education courses are four-session programs costing $50 per person or household, covering mortgages, credit, down payments, home selection, and landlord-tenant relations. Upon completion and submission of assignments, participants receive certificates valid for two years.

CSN distinguishes itself through multiple course delivery formats to accommodate different learning styles and schedules, partnerships with local financial institutions (StonehamBank, Rockland Trust) and established platforms (Framework, My Mass Home, CHAPA), and a philosophy centered on education and dignity rather than hand-outs. The organization emphasizes client empowerment through knowledge-building and solution-finding. Their face-to-face courses are limited to 20-25 participants to maintain quality instruction, and classes run for three and a half hours across four sessions.

A key limitation is that CSN's direct monetary assistance (STAF program) appears limited to Stoneham homeowners specifically, though educational services extend statewide. The organization relies on donations and sponsorships to fund programs. Course registration deadlines are strict with pre-registration required, and class capacity is limited. The website provides limited information about program outcomes or impact metrics, and some service areas show no current scheduling (webinar courses have no next date listed).
pros: Multiple course delivery formats: in-person classes, webinars, and online hybrid courses with Framework to accommodate different needs and schedules
Low cost at $50 per person or household for comprehensive four-session homebuyer education covering credit, mortgages, down payments, and home selection
Pre-purchase courses result in certificates valid for two years, which may be required by some lenders
Partnerships with local banks (StonehamBank, Rockland Trust) and established platforms (My Mass Home, CHAPA) for course scheduling and credibility
Stoneham Tax Assistance Fund provides direct monetary assistance for property taxes to qualifying low-to-moderate income homeowners
Advocacy and referral services connecting clients to additional resources like disability grants
Small class sizes (20-25 participants maximum) enabling personalized attention and instructor engagement
cons: STAF program's direct monetary assistance appears limited to Stoneham residents only, restricting access to their most substantial offering
Strict pre-registration requirements and early deadlines (must register by noon Thursday before course week) create barriers to participation
No webinar courses currently scheduled; next offering date is unspecified, limiting accessibility for remote learners
Limited information on website about program outcomes, participant demographics served, or impact metrics
Courses are limited to first-time homebuyers; existing homeowners cannot access homebuyer education programs
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Massachusetts seeking affordable pre-purchase education and financial preparation
Low-to-moderate income Stoneham homeowners struggling with property tax burden and seeking monetary assistance
Individuals unable to attend in-person classes who prefer online or webinar-based homebuyer education
diagnosis: CSN is best for first-time homebuyers and low-to-moderate income Massachusetts residents seeking affordable, non-profit education and housing assistance. The main caveat is that direct monetary assistance (STAF) is limited to Stoneham homeowners, and potential participants must commit to strict pre-registration deadlines well in advance of course dates.
services: Stoneham Tax Assistance Fund (STAF) - monetary assistance for property taxes for low-to-moderate income homeowners
Face-to-face pre-purchase homebuyer education courses (4 sessions, $50, held Mondays and Wednesdays 6-9:30 PM)
Online hybrid pre-purchase homebuyer course in partnership with Framework
Pre-purchase homebuyer education covering mortgages, credit, down payments, and home selection
Landlord-tenant education for multi-family property purchases
Pre-purchase homebuyer course completion certificates valid for two years
Advocacy and referral services to grants and resources
Financial education to help clients improve standard of living
Information gathering and solution-finding assistance for housing-related issues
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
